title: Molybdenum : Part-VII. Dioxo Molybdenum (VI) Complexes with Benzoyl Phenyl Hydroxylamine or Substituted Hydroxylamine
author: R. L. Dutta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Reaction of (NH 4 ) 2 [MoOCI 5 ] and benzoylphenyl hydroxylamine (or substituted hydroxylammes) in ethanol leads to the syntheses of bis N-Benzoylphenyl hydroxylamine -dioxo-molybdenum (VI) and related chelates. These are cream coloured, diamagnetic and their solution spectra reveal no ligand field bands in the visible region.
steps_raw: Reaction of (NH 4 ) 2 [MoOCI 5 ] and benzoylphenyl hydroxylamine (or substituted hydroxylammes) in ethanol leads to the syntheses of bis N-Benzoylphenyl hydroxylamine -dioxo-molybdenum (VI) and related chelates. These are cream coloured, diamagnetic and their solution spectra reveal no ligand field bands in the visible region.
